fields.title: Future of Team Workflows
fields.subtitle: Why source-native collaboration changes how teams ship.

fields.title: The shift
fields.subtitle: From static documents to editable, inspectable HTML artifacts.

slides.title: Source-native decks are easier to trust

fields.title: Source-native decks are easier to trust
points: HTML stays readable in version control.
Editors can preserve original structure instead of flattening it.
Teams can review content and layout in the same artifact.

fields.title: Protocol before automation
fields.body: A stable document contract makes generation, editing, validation, and rendering part of the same workflow instead of separate conversions.
points: The style pack defines visual slices.
Tools validate and normalize the output.

leftPoints: Import into a private document model
rightPoints: Keep HTML as the source of truth
Edit and validate the same files
fields.summary: The contract should reduce translation boundaries, not add more of them.

slides.title: What teams need from deck tooling

fields.title: What teams need from deck tooling
points: A visual system that covers common page forms

fields.title: Adoption path
milestones: Phase 2: ship a starter style pack
Phase 3: add validation and annotation tools

fields.title: Why protocol coverage matters
fields.note: The protocol should stay smaller than the style surface built on top of it.

slides.title: The editor should understand real slides

fields.title: The editor should understand real slides
fields.caption: Media-heavy layouts should still expose clear editable zones.

slides.title: A good style pack makes the protocol feel invisible

fields.title: A good style pack makes the protocol feel invisible
fields.body: Authors should feel like they are choosing a design language, while the system quietly preserves editability underneath it.
fields.action: Build style packs against the specimen deck, then map user content into those same archetypes.
